name: Pagination & Loading
description: How pagination and progressive loading should support browsing large catalogues without frustration or disorientation

heuristics.principle: The pagination method should be clearly communicated so users understand how to access more products
checkpoints: Pagination controls are visible below the product grid
Current page position within total results is indicated
Next, previous, and direct page number navigation is available
Users can identify how many total products or pages exist

heuristics.principle: Infinite scroll or load-more should preserve the user's position and provide a sense of progress
checkpoints: Loading indicator appears when fetching more products
Scroll position is preserved without jumping
A count of loaded vs total products is shown
Users can still access the footer without being blocked by endless loading

heuristics.principle: Returning from a PDP should restore the user's previous position in the listing
checkpoints: Browser back button returns to the same scroll position
Previously loaded products are still visible without re-fetching
Applied filters and sort order are preserved
The product the user clicked on is identifiable in context

heuristics.principle: Initial page load should show products quickly without excessive skeleton screens or loading delays
checkpoints: First batch of product cards renders within 2-3 seconds
Skeleton loaders or placeholders indicate content is loading
Images load progressively without causing layout shifts
Above-fold products are prioritised for loading
